mnemonics: Catch Order Matters: Specific to General (SOG)
keyPoints: Catch blocks are evaluated from top to bottom; the first compatible exception type is executed.
You must order catch blocks from the most specific exception type to the most general (e.g., FileNotFoundException before IOException).
A single try block can be followed by multiple catch blocks to handle different types of exceptions separately.
commonMistakes: Placing a catch block for a general exception (like Exception) before a specific one, which makes the specific catch block unreachable.
Catching an exception type that is never thrown by the code in the try block, which is a compile-time error.
Using multiple catch blocks unnecessarily when one more general catch block would suffice, making the code less readable.
